news_summary: France's National Library has revitalized a major cultural treasure by uncovering and restoring a forgotten Mozart manuscript.
news_summary_fr: La Bibliothèque nationale de France a redonné vie à un trésor culturel majeur en mettant au jour et en restaurant un manuscrit oublié de Mozart.
news_summary_es: La Biblioteca Nacional de Francia ha devuelto a la luz un importante tesoro cultural al descubrir y restaurar un manuscrito olvidado de Mozart.

articles.title: 'Major discovery': France's National Library brings forgotten Mozart manuscript back to life
articles.summary: A long-forgotten manuscript by composer Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart will be brought to life this weekend in Paris. The newly rediscovered work – composed in 1778 when the Austrian prodigy was just 22 – will be performed in public for the first time ever at France's National Library.
articles.content:
Musicians this weekend will for the first time publicly interpret music for flute and harp that Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart wrote as a 22-year-old while teaching an aristocratic French student.

The unprecedented concert on Sunday at France's National Library (BnF) comes after what it has called a "major discovery".

Francois-Pierre Goy, a curator in the library's music department, stumbled across the treasure as he examined a pile of anonymous manuscripts he wanted to get through before retirement.

"I never imagined what I was about to find," he told AFP.

The 44-page notebook includes a dozen daily exercises the Austrian prodigy gave Marie-Louise-Philippine de Bonnieres de Guines from May to July 1778, as well as seven pieces for flute and harp, he said.

She was an excellent harpist and the daughter of the Duke of Guines, himself a renowned flautist.

"It just so happened that I had been looking at some of Mozart's teaching material a few weeks earlier," Goy said.

Soon he noticed similarities – including "the treble clefs that are quite rounded and tilted slightly forward", and bass clefs drawn in the opposite direction from how they usually are in France, he added.

"Could it be him?" Goy said he thought to himself.

Comparisons with Mozart's other handwritten works, the French paper used, and stamps on the notebook identical to those on a French copy of Mozart's "Concerto for Flute and Harp" that the Duke of Guines had commissioned all seemed to indicate he was right.

Armin Brinzing, director of the Austria-based Mozarteum Foundation, authenticated the document in April.

The manuscript "is part of two bundles of music that were confiscated from the home of the Duke of Guines in 1794" during the French Revolution, and eventually ended up at the BnF, according to the library.

Mozart died in 1791 aged 35.

Discoveries like this "for such a famous composer are almost unheard of", said Mathias Auclair, director of the BnF's music department.

Several Mozart compositions have been rediscovered in recent years.

Read moreMozart Foundation discovers two new compositions

In one case, in 2012, someone found a Mozart piano piece composed when he was 11 in an Austrian attic.

For harpists and flautists, who have "very little repertoire" available to them, the discovery at the BnF is a wonderful surprise, he said.

BnF president Gilles Pecout said the new music sheets shed light on Mozart as a young teacher and documented his last stay in Paris in 1778 – on which there is scant information.

(FRANCE 24 with AFP)
articles.source: News and stories on CULTURE, movies, music, literature - France 24
articles.reasoning: The rediscovery of a forgotten Mozart manuscript represents a significant cultural and historical achievement with broad public interest and educational value. It brings new music to life, enriching the repertoire for musicians and deepening understanding of Mozart's life and work. The story is focused, detailed, and highlights a tangible positive impact on arts and culture.
articles.personality_title: France's National Library unveils lost Mozart music after centuries
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, a famous composer from Austria, wrote many pieces of music in the 1700s. Some of his works were lost or forgotten over time.

**What happened** – This weekend, musicians in Paris will perform a newly found Mozart manuscript for flute and harp. The music was written in 1778 when Mozart was 22 years old. The manuscript was discovered by Francois-Pierre Goy, a curator at France’s National Library, while sorting through old papers before his retirement. He recognized Mozart’s handwriting and style, and experts confirmed the find.

**Impact** – This discovery is rare because finding new works by Mozart is almost unheard of. The music adds new pieces to the limited flute and harp repertoire and gives us a glimpse into Mozart’s time teaching a French noblewoman. It also helps us learn more about his life during his visit to Paris in 1778.

**What’s next step** – The manuscript will be performed publicly for the first time, bringing the music to life after centuries. Scholars and musicians will study the work further, and it may inspire more concerts or research about Mozart’s teaching and compositions.

**One-sentence takeaway** – A forgotten Mozart manuscript from 1778 was found in France and will be played publicly for the first time, enriching music history and performance.
articles.personality_title_fr: La Bibliothèque nationale de France révèle une musique perdue de Mozart après des siècles
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, un célèbre compositeur autrichien du XVIIIe siècle, a écrit de nombreuses œuvres musicales. Certaines de ses partitions ont été perdues ou oubliées avec le temps.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Ce week-end, des musiciens à Paris joueront une partition de Mozart récemment retrouvée pour flûte et harpe. Cette musique date de 1778, quand Mozart avait 22 ans. Le manuscrit a été découvert par François-Pierre Goy, conservateur à la Bibliothèque nationale de France, en triant de vieux documents avant sa retraite. Il a reconnu l’écriture et le style de Mozart, ce que des experts ont confirmé.

**Impact** – Cette découverte est rare car il est presque impossible de trouver de nouvelles œuvres de Mozart. La musique enrichit le répertoire limité pour flûte et harpe et nous offre un aperçu de Mozart lorsqu’il enseignait à une noble française. Elle aide aussi à mieux comprendre sa vie lors de son séjour à Paris en 1778.

**Prochaine étape** – Le manuscrit sera joué en public pour la première fois, donnant vie à cette musique après plusieurs siècles. Les chercheurs et musiciens vont étudier cette œuvre, qui pourrait inspirer d’autres concerts ou recherches sur l’enseignement et les compositions de Mozart.

**En une phrase** – Une partition oubliée de Mozart datant de 1778 a été retrouvée en France et sera jouée en public pour la première fois, enrichissant l’histoire et la pratique musicales.
articles.personality_title_es: La Biblioteca Nacional de Francia revela música perdida de Mozart tras siglos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, un famoso compositor austríaco del siglo XVIII, escribió muchas obras musicales. Algunas de sus partituras se perdieron u olvidaron con el tiempo.

**Qué pasó** – Este fin de semana, músicos en París tocarán una partitura de Mozart recién encontrada para flauta y arpa. La música fue escrita en 1778, cuando Mozart tenía 22 años. El manuscrito fue descubierto por François-Pierre Goy, un curador de la Biblioteca Nacional de Francia, mientras revisaba documentos antiguos antes de retirarse. Reconoció la escritura y el estilo de Mozart, y expertos confirmaron el hallazgo.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento es raro porque encontrar nuevas obras de Mozart es casi imposible. La música añade nuevas piezas al limitado repertorio para flauta y arpa y nos da una visión de Mozart mientras enseñaba a una noble francesa. También ayuda a entender mejor su vida durante su visita a París en 1778.

**Próximo paso** – El manuscrito se interpretará en público por primera vez, dando vida a la música después de siglos. Académicos y músicos estudiarán la obra y podría inspirar más conciertos o investigaciones sobre la enseñanza y composiciones de Mozart.

**Una frase resumen** – Una partitura olvidada de Mozart de 1778 fue encontrada en Francia y se tocará en público por primera vez, enriqueciendo la historia y la interpretación musical.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of an open antique music manuscript resting gently on a wooden table, with delicate flute and harp silhouettes emerging softly from the pages as ethereal musical notes swirl around, set against the subtle backdrop of a grand old library interior bathed in natural, muted tones.
